prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿弹跳" (straight-leg bounding) exercise, side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style. Person posture: Photographic freeze-frame of a STRAIGHT-LEG bounding drill, side view. The athlete is suspended IN MID-AIR — about 15-25 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below both feet (a small shadow on the ground far below the body confirms airborne position). BOTH KNEES ARE LOCKED COMPLETELY STRAIGHT — zero knee bend, both legs RIGID like sticks. The FRONT (left) leg is locked-out and angled forward at about 30 degrees from vertical with the foot DORSIFLEXED (toes pulled UP). The REAR (right) leg is locked-out and angled back at about 30 degrees from vertical with the foot PLANTARFLEXED (toes pointed DOWN). Together the two stiff-straight legs form a wide scissor pattern in the air. Arms swinging in vigorous running-style opposition. Body traveling forward (rightward). Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT running with bent knees — both knees are FULLY STRAIGHT throughout the movement; both feet are OFF THE GROUND.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and hamstring group. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and quadriceps. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "臀大肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the glute and quadriceps area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
